places.name: Lingshan Giant Buddha
places.description: The Lingshan Giant Buddha is an 88-meter tall bronze statue of Maitreya Buddha located in the Lingshan Scenic Area, Wuxi, Jiangsu Province, China. Completed in 1996, it is one of the largest Buddha statues in the world and a major Buddhist pilgrimage site.
highlights: Brahma Palace - a magnificent Buddhist temple complex
Nine Dragons Fountain - spectacular water and light show
places.ticket_info: Entrance fee includes access to all attractions within the area
places.access: Bus or taxi from Wuxi city center

places.name: Uantouzhu Scenic Area
places.location: Northern shore of Taihu Lake, Wuxi
places.description: Uantouzhu, also known as Turtle Head Peninsula, is a scenic area located on the northern shore of Taihu Lake in Wuxi. It gets its name from its shape, which resembles a turtle's head extending into the lake. The area is famous for its beautiful cherry blossoms in spring.
highlights: Spring cherry blossoms (late March to early April)
places.ticket_info: Entrance fee includes access to all attractions within the area
places.access: Bus or taxi from Wuxi city center

places.name: Nanchang Street
places.location: Center of Wuxi, along the Grand Canal
places.description: Nanchang Street is a historic street located in the center of Wuxi, running along the banks of the Grand Canal. With a history dating back over 1,000 years, it is one of the best-preserved ancient streets in the city.
highlights: Shops selling traditional crafts and souvenirs
Restaurants and teahouses serving local cuisine
Grand Canal views and stone bridges
places.opening_hours: 24 hours (shops and restaurants typically 10:00 AM - 10:00 PM)
places.access: Walking distance from city center, bus or taxi

places.name: Taihu Lake
places.description: Taihu Lake, also known as Lake Tai, is the third largest freshwater lake in China, located in the Yangtze River Delta. It covers an area of about 2,250 square kilometers and is shared by the provinces of Jiangsu and Zhejiang.
places.access: Bus or taxi to various entry points

places.name: Huishan Ancient Town
places.description: Huishan Ancient Town is a well-preserved ancient town located in the western part of Wuxi. With a history dating back over 3,000 years, it is one of the oldest towns in the region and a significant cultural heritage site.
highlights: Huishan Temple - built during the Eastern Jin Dynasty
places.ticket_info: Entrance fee includes access to most attractions
places.access: Bus or taxi from Wuxi city center
